title: Has v1.18 been released recently? About when?
body: Has v1.18 been released recently? About when?

comments.body:
> Aiming for this week

ok， I am still in version 1.16 and do not want to upgrade to version 1.17. I will wait for version 1.18 to update.
I believe the recommended guidance is to upgrade in order. Might I ask why you don't want to upgrade to 1.17?

> I believe the recommended guidance is to upgrade in order. Might I ask why you don't want to upgrade to 1.17?

Because I integrated 1.16, but didn't actually use it. It's too difficult for small companies to deploy with only three servers.
So I want to wait until 1.18 to see if there are any improvements.

We've got several pretty straightforward guides [published](https://docs.dapr.io/operations/hosting/kubernetes/cluster/) and unless you're really getting into the weeds of the customization, it really shouldn't be _that_ complicated to set up. To my knowledge, nothing is changing on the install front - it's workflow and actor updates, some new components and some CLI improvements.

Do feel free to reach out on our [Discord channel](http://bit.ly/dapr-discord) if you're having any issues - I or someone else can certainly try to chime in and set you on the right path.

> We've got several pretty straightforward guides [published](https://docs.dapr.io/operations/hosting/kubernetes/cluster/) and unless you're really getting into the weeds of the customization, it really shouldn't be _that_ complicated to set up. To my knowledge, nothing is changing on the install front - it's workflow and actor updates, some new components and some CLI improvements.
> 
> Do feel free to reach out on our [Discord channel](http://bit.ly/dapr-discord) if you're having any issues - I or someone else can certainly try to chime in and set you on the right path.

I looked at it and it was the same issue before. Without k8s, I had to configure and deploy three internal network servers on my own, which seemed difficult to implement. My program was deployed using Docker Composer by myself.

It is certainly intended to be used as a sidecar pattern using an orchestrator like Kubernetes, but there are a few alternative deployment styles. I've heard of several people that use [Docker Compose](https://docs.dapr.io/operations/hosting/self-hosted/self-hosted-with-docker/#run-using-docker-compose).  If the setup itself is a little too much, you might also look at using [Diagrid's Catalyst](https://www.diagrid.io/catalyst) solution - it's a hosted version of Dapr so all you need to do is configure and connect to it, no local setup required.
